calendar.matters:
Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust
Off Calendar Jul-22-2011 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gerard Lam for Tian-Wei Liu. Chang Yu in Propria Persona and Lance Yu in Propria Persona (Successor Co-Trustees).

The Parties presented oral argument to support their positions. The matter was taken under submission. The Court requested a proposed order from Ms. Lam's office and a propsed order from Mr. Yu and Ms. Chow denying the petition.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy A. Payne.

Petition To Determine Validity Of Trust
Off Calendar May-23-2011 Continued To Jul-11-11 At 09:00 A.M. In Dept 204
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: Gerard Lam for and with Tian-Wei Liu. Chang Yu in Propria Persona. Lance Chou in Propria Persona.

Mr. Lam stated that the Trust was premised on the erroneous belief that the Trustors did not have any Children, that an adoption took place in 1980 in China, that there are immigration papers showing that Mr. Liu was adopted before he was admitted as a permanent resident in the United States, that he has all four amendments and that the School was given notice. In addition, Mr. Lam stated that they did not have all of the addresses or identities of all of the Beneficiaries and that he needed time to do discovery. The Court directed Mr. Lam to do an amended petition, to attach all of the Trust amendments, to epxlain the adoption and to re-notice everybody. The matter was continued to July 11, 2011 at 9:00 a.m., in Dept. 204. Chang Yu and Lance Chou are to file responses.

Judge Pro Temp: Sue M. Kaplan. Reporter: Diana Chen, CSR#11312. Clerk: Rosie Noguera. Bailiff: Deputy R. Cabrera.
